case.title: ATLANTIC GULF & PACIFIC CO. v. GEROSA

opinion.h4: ATLANTIC GULF & PACIFIC CO. v. GEROSA, (1966)
ATLANTIC GULF & PACIFIC CO. v. GEROSA, COMPTROLLER OF THE CITY OF NEW YORK. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF APPEALS OF NEW YORK. No. 718. Decided January 17, 1966.
opinion.p: Leo A. Larkin, Stanley Buchsbaum and Samuel J. Warms for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
opinion.h4: PENNSYLVANIA RAILROAD CO. v. UNITED STATES, (1966)
opinion.p: PENNSYLVANIA RAILROAD CO. ET AL. v. UNITED STATES ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE EASTERN DISTRICT OF PENNSYLVANIA. No. 556. Decided January 17, 1966.
Paul R. Duke and Edward A. Kaier for appellants.

Solicitor General Marshall, Assistant Attorney General Turner, Robert B. Hummel, Neil Brooks, Robert W. Ginnane and I. K. Hay for the United States et al. Leo A. Larkin, Samuel Mandell, Sidney Goldstein, F. A. Mulhern, Arthur L. Winn, Jr., Samuel H. Moerman, J. Raymond Clark and James M. Henderson for appellee Port of New York Authority.
The motions to affirm are granted and the judgment is affirmed.
